title: Composure Before the Stage
intention: To meet pre-performance nerves with Stoic clarity, grounding what is mine and releasing what is not, so adrenaline becomes steady presence.

lines.text: Welcome. Take this moment, before the moment, and make it your own.
Feel your feet on the ground, and the ground holding you up.
Let your breath come in. Let it go without effort.
Notice the energy in your chest, your hands, your pulse. This is not fear. This is power arriving.
The body cannot tell excitement from dread. So name it well. Call it readiness.
Now, a clear line. Some things are up to you, and some are not.
The outcome, the verdict, the faces in the crowd. These are not in your hands. Set them down.
What remains is yours alone. Your attention. Your effort. The next breath, the next note, the next word.
Breathe in, and gather only what is yours.
Breathe out, and release what was never yours to carry.
You have prepared. The work is done. All that is asked of you now is to be here.
Whatever comes, you will meet it as it is, not as you fear it.
Let the shoulders drop. Let the jaw soften. The strength you need is quiet.
Feel the steadiness settle low in your body, like a stone that will not be moved.
When you are ready, lift your eyes. The energy is yours to use.
Step forward, grounded and awake. Begin.
